Ad Text:
Summary

As a Data Engineer at Avanade, you’ll design and build scalable data pipelines and ETL/ELT solutions. You’ll work closely with architects and analysts to deliver secure, high‑quality data across batch and near‑real‑time use cases, while continuously improving performance and reliability.




Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and data integration solutions

Develop and optimise ETL/ELT processes to ingest, transform, and store data from multiple sources

Work closely with data architects, analysts, and downstream consumers to deliver reliable data solutions

Implement data processing solutions across batch and near-real-time use cases

Ensure data quality, reliability, and performance across data platforms

Support cloud-based data platforms, data lakes, and data warehouses

Apply data security, privacy, and access control requirements across data solutions

Troubleshoot and resolve data pipeline failures and performance issues

Contribute to automation, monitoring, and continuous improvement of data engineering practices




Skills and Experience

Strong experience in Data Engineering or data-focused engineering roles

Strong experience with ETL/ELT development and data pipeline orchestration

Solid proficiency in SQL and experience with relational and NoSQL data stores

Experience with cloud data platforms (e.g. Azure Data Factory, Databricks, Synapse or equivalent)

Experience working with structured and semi-structured data

Familiarity with data modelling concepts and performance optimisation techniques

Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices for data solutions

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills

About Accenture

Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Find out more about us at www.accenture.com.




What you’ll do 

We are seeking a Senior Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) to lead the design and deployment of advanced AI solutions for enterprise customers. This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product, and customer success, requiring deep technical expertise, strategic thinking, and strong client engagement skills.

As a senior member of the team, you will own end-to-end delivery of complex implementations, influence architectural decisions, mentor engineers, and act as a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders. You will leverage leading large language models (LLMs), including Anthropic’s Claude (via Anthropic Vault), OpenAI’s GPT, and Google’s Gemini, to build secure, scalable, and high-impact solutions.




Key responsibilities

Strategic Client Leadership:

- Serve as the primary technical advisor for enterprise clients.

- Lead discovery workshops to identify high-value AI use cases.

- Drive end-to-end delivery of complex deployments.

- Influence customer roadmaps and long-term AI strategies.




Solution Architecture & Delivery:

- Architect and implement enterprise-grade AI systems leveraging Anthropic Vault, OpenAI GPT, and Google Gemini.

- Design secure and scalable Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines and agentic workflows.

- Integrate LLMs with enterprise data sources.

- Establish best practices for performance, reliability, cost optimization, and governance.




Technical Leadership & Mentorship:

- Mentor and guide junior engineers.

- Contribute to internal frameworks and reference architectures.

- Provide leadership during complex troubleshooting and incident resolution.




Operational Excellence & Governance:

- Define evaluation frameworks for model quality and safety.

- Ensure compliance with enterprise security and regulatory requirements.

- Implement monitoring, observability, and LLMOps practices.




Here’s what you need

Education & Experience:

- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

- 7–10+ years of experience in software engineering, solutions architecture, or technical consulting.

- 3+ years delivering AI/ML or LLM-based solutions in production.

- Proven track record of leading complex, customer-facing technical engagements.




Technical Skills:

Programming & Engineering:

- Expert-level proficiency in Python and strong experience with JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, or Go.

- Deep understanding of distributed systems, microservices, and API design.

- Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.




LLM & AI Platforms:

- Hands-on experience with Anthropic Vault, OpenAI GPT, and Google Gemini.

- Expertise in prompt engineering, embeddings, vector databases, and RAG architectures.




Cloud & Data Technologies:

- Strong experience with AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.

- Familiarity with data platforms such as Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks.

- Experience with vector databases such as Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS, or Milvus.




Bonus points if with

- Experience deploying AI solutions in regulated industries such as finance, healthcare, or government

- Familiarity with responsible AI and model risk management frameworks

- Contributions to open-source projects or technical publications

- Experience with evaluation and observability tools such as LangSmith, Promptfoo, or Weights & Biases

- Prior experience in a startup or high-growth technology environment




Benefits of working at Accenture:

·       18 weeks paid parental leave

·       Long & short-term career break opportunities

·       Structured career development program

·       Local and international career opportunities.

·       Certified as a Family Inclusive Workplace™

·       Flexible Work Arrangements - centered around Accenture’s Truly Human ethos and our commitment to supporting the health and wellbeing of our people.

·       We are proud to be in the top 3 of last year’s Diversity & Inclusion Index!




We are a WORK180 Endorsed Employer, to see our benefits and policies click here.




All our consulting professionals receive comprehensive training covering business acumen, technical and professional skills development. You’ll also have opportunities to hone your functional skills and expertise in an area of specialization. We offer a variety of formal and informal training programs at every level to help you acquire and build specialized skills faster. Learning takes place both on the job and through formal training conducted online, in the classroom, or in collaboration with teammates. The sheer variety of work we do, and the experience it offers, provide an unbeatable platform from which to build a career.




Equal Employment Opportunity

At Accenture, we recognise that our people are multi-dimensional, and we create a work environment where all people feel like they can bring their authentic selves to work, every day.




Our unwavering commitment to inclusion and diversity unleashes innovation and creates a culture where everyone feels they have equal opportunity. Our range of progressive policies support flexibility in ‘where’, ‘when’ and ‘how’ our people work to ensure that Accenture is an organisation where you can strive for more, achieve great things and maintain the balance and wellbeing you need.




We encourage applications from all people, and we are committed to removing barriers to the recruitment process and employee lifecycle. All employment decisions shall be made without regard to age, disability status, ethnicity, gender, gender identity or expression, religion or sexual orientation and we do not tolerate discrimination. If you require adjustments to the recruitment process or have a preferred communication method, please email exectalent@accenture.com and cite the relevant Job Number, or contact us on +61 2 9005 5000.




To ensure our workplace is inclusive and diverse we are setting bold goals and taking comprehensive action. To achieve these goals, we collect information that allows us to track the effectiveness of our Inclusion and Diversity programs. As an AI Technical Lead, you will own and evolve Silverchain's enterprise AI platform and technical delivery of AI to enable safe, cost-effective, and scalable delivery of AI and GenAI solutions across care delivery, corporate, and operational functions. Combine architecture leadership with hands-on enablement to accelerate value, ensure compliance, and uplift team capability.

This is a highly influential role where your ability to connect people, uplift capability, and lead meaningful change will directly impact the future of how Silverchain delivers Best Care in homes and communities nationwide.

What's on offer

Fulltime One-year Fixed Term role. Monday-Friday 76 hours per fortnight.
Hybrid role - working from home, location preferred Perth.
Working for a non-profit organisation, you can salary package up to $18,500 tax free.
Everyday discounts, including discounted vouchers for Coles, Woolworths, Amazon, Myer, The Good Guys and more.
Comprehensive health and wellbeing resources and support.

What we're looking for

To thrive in this role you'll need to have:

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence or related fields.
5+ years across software/data/AI and or designing and operating AI/ML platforms at scale.
Strong business writing and presentation skills.
Highly organised, efficient, with exceptional interpersonal skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills.
Familiarity with Agile ways of working, and the ability to scope, develop, implement, measure and report on innovation activities with agility.
Familiarity with Australian Privacy Act (APPs), clinical governance principles.

Technical:

Cloud (Azure preferred; AWS/GCP ok).
Containers & orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes/AKS)
MLOps/LLMOps (Azure ML/MLflow, model registry, feature store, vector DBs such as Azure AI Search)
CI/CD (GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps), testing strategies, policy-as-code
Observability & model monitoring
GenAI patterns (RAG, prompt engineering, evaluators, guardrails, content filters; agents/orchestration).

The role

As an AI Technical Lead:

Define the end-to-end AI reference architecture (data, models, APIs, RAG, vector search, observability, security, governance).
Select and standardise core platform components (e.g., model hosting, vector DB, feature store, prompt management, experiment tracking, model registry, CI/CD, evaluation harness, guardrails, monitoring).
Establish environment topology (dev/test/stage/prod), multi-tenant patterns, and networking/security baselines.
Support stand-up the platform (cloud resources, clusters, registries, monitoring, cost allocation tags).
Design CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps) for reproducible builds for non-prod.
Provide technical leadership and coaching; run design reviews and guilds.
Curate standards and playbooks; champion secure-by-design and privacy-by-default.

Why choose Silverchain

Everyone at Silverchain, from our care teams to leadership, plays a vital role in delivering Best Care. It's a team effort. Best Care is care that is safe, personal, connected and effective.

We are proudly one of the few Australian home care providers accredited in both national health and aged care standards. This means the quality of care we provide is expected to meet the same standards as a hospital or residential aged care facility. As a not-for-profit organisation, we re-invest our funds into research and developing innovative models of care to set the industry benchmark for the provision of the best care in the home.

We'll also provide you with opportunities to help you develop and grow in your career, with training and education programs to help you achieve your goals.

How to apply

To submit your application, click ‘Apply'.

At Silverchain, we recognise the positive contribution that a diverse workforce makes to our organisation where individuals can be authentic, are respected and celebrated. We strongly encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people of all ages, gender, abilities, culturally diverse and linguistic backgrounds.

Build the Ecosystem. Govern the Future.

Temple & Webster is where design meets technology. As Australia’s leading online retailer for furniture and homewares, we aren’t just selling products; we are reimagining how people discover and shop for their homes. We are looking for an Agentic AI Platforms Engineer to serve as the custodian of our autonomous agent ecosystem, ensuring high-impact solutions operate within a unified framework of governance and excellence.

The Role

This is a pivotal position for a technologist who wants to move beyond individual tools and architect the platform that powers them. You will be the primary custodian of our Agent Registry, standardising how business and technical agents are deployed to ensure they share a common "Source of Truth". You’ll define the "Rules of Engagement," manage security configurations, and protect the company from "Agentic Drift" as we scale our AI capabilities.

What we’re looking for

We value creative thinking and a builder’s mindset over a rigid checklist. We’re interested in:

Platform Curiosity: You don’t just build agents; you think about the infrastructure they live on. You’re interested in how Model Context Protocol (MCP) can connect agents to internal data and tools to erase duplication.
The "Iterative Architect" Mindset: We move fast. You should be comfortable with rapid experimentation - learning quickly from each deployment and relentlessly refining systems to meet performance benchmarks.
Communication & Collaboration: You can bridge the gap between complex engineering and business impact. You’ll partner with AI Engineers, Data Scientists, and tech teams to translate "tribal knowledge" into robust agentic logic.
Technical Depth: You have a background in software, DevOps, or ML engineering. You’re proficient in Python and Terraform, and you have experience deploying and monitoring agentic systems in cloud environments (we love GCP).
What you’ll be doing
Guardian of the Ecosystem: Owning the lifecycle, integrity, and safety of our autonomous agents.
Standardising Deployment: Managing agent environments using GitOps principles—versioning prompts, instructions, and schemas as production code.
Maintaining Excellence: Performing BAU maintenance and prototyping aggressively while upholding the security and architectural standards required for enterprise deployment.
Why Temple & Webster?
Real Impact: Join a tight-knit team where your work directly influences the experience of millions of customers.
Innovation-First Culture: We believe in using the right tool for the job. You'll have the space to experiment with Google, OpenAI, Anthropic and the latest AI frameworks to solve real-world retail problems.
The T&W Way: Our culture is built on our people. We provide a beautiful workspace in St Peters, hybrid flexibility, catered lunches three days a week, and a genuine focus on wellbeing (including daily mindfulness and birthday leave) amongst many benefits.
Ready to architect the future? If you’re a systems-thinker who loves building the infrastructure that makes innovation possible, we’d love to hear from you. Hit 'Apply' to start the conversation.

Advanced Navigation is the world’s most determined innovator in AI robotics and navigation technologies across land, air, sea and space applications. Founded on a culture of research and discovery, Advanced Navigation's mission is to be the catalyst of the autonomy revolution. Fields of expertise include artificial intelligence, underwater sonar, GNSS, radio frequency systems, inertial sensors, robotics, quantum sensors and photonics. 

Today, Advanced Navigation is a supplier to some of the world's largest companies, including Airbus, Boeing, Google, Tesla, NASA, Apple, and General Motors. Discover it for yourself: https://youtu.be/F7uAqs1gtuk

OVERVIEW OF ROLE 

In this role you will be a subject matter expert, filling a critical knowledge gap in your engineering team. Given that our company heavily relies on software to enhance hardware performance, this person must have a strong multidisciplinary background across physics, electronics, and software.

The ideal candidate would have a unique blend of hands-on testing experience and a deep theoretical understanding of sensor technologies. Your background should bridge the gap between our software, mechanical, and electronic engineering teams.


ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES 

Contribute to the research and development of novel, internally-developed sensor technologies.
Provide expert guidance to the engineering team on the critical properties of sensors for inertial navigation systems.
Develop and implement advanced sensor calibration techniques to optimise system performance.
Lead the testing, characterisation, and selection of third-party inertial sensors.
Analyse test data to identify sensor performance limitations and areas for improvement.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate sensors into our diverse product range, from low-cost to high-end systems.

QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE & SKILLS 

Educational Background: A degree in a relevant field such as Physics, Mechatronics, Electrical Engineering, or a closely related discipline is essential. An advanced degree (Master's or PhD) would be highly advantageous, especially if their research was focused on sensor technology or inertial navigation.

Sensor Testing and Characterisation: They must have proven experience in designing and executing tests for inertial sensors (accelerometers and gyroscopes). This includes understanding test methodologies, sources of error, and how to analyse the resulting data.

Sensor Development: Experience in the development of new sensors is crucial. This could involve technologies like MEMS (Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems), FOG (Fibre-Optic Gyroscopes), or other emerging sensor tech. This experience ensures they can contribute to your in-house sensor development.

Inertial Navigation Systems (INS) Expertise: A deep understanding of how sensor characteristics impact the performance of an INS is paramount. They should be able to identify which sensor properties are most critical for your high-performance navigation systems.

Calibration Techniques: The candidate should be proficient in advanced sensor calibration methods to minimise errors and optimise performance.

Software and Data Analysis Skills: Proficiency in software tools used for data acquisition and analysis, such as Python or MATLAB, is important.
Industry Experience: While not mandatory, experience in one of our key industries—like defense, maritime, or autonomous vehicles—would be a significant plus, as they would

Passionate about building and deploying Machine Learning pipelines at scale to drive business value? Join our growing Data Science Team as our first Senior Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) Engineer!

What's in it for you?

As a Senior MLOps Engineer, you will work within our collaborative Data Science team to help deliver and accelerate multiple machine learning projects across our organisation.

Your role with us:

In your role with us, you will enhance our machine learning operations (MLOps), delivering: robust, scalable AWS cloud infrastructure and automation solutions, that empower our data science team. You will get the opportunity to work with petabyte-scale data across our global platforms, directly impacting millions of users.

Who are we?

At Easygo we proudly stand as a prominent service provider to a powerhouse of brands within the entertainment industry, including Stake.com, Kick.com and Twist Gaming. 

Stake is the world's largest crypto casino, and leads the industry with a seamless online casino and sportsbook experience.

Level up your online entertainment with Kick.com, the vibrant live-streaming platform, which connects millions of gamers and content creators worldwide.

All alongside the innovative game design studio, Twist Gaming, which takes creativity to new heights by crafting cutting-edge and captivating games. 

Our commitment to placing our clients and their communities' entertainment at the forefront of everything we do, has solidified us as the ultimate online service provider for entertainment companies.

Headquartered in the beautiful city of Melbourne, our growth has been remarkable. From humble beginnings to a thriving workforce of 700+, we've expanded not only in numbers but in ambition. There really is something for everyone here, whether you work in Tech, Marketing, Operations, Mathematics or Design, we are sure to have something for everyone.

Click play, on your career today!

What you will do:

Lead the design, implementation, and maintenance of end-to-end ML infrastructure and automation solutions, from: development, to deployment and production monitoring.
Drive cloud infrastructure and architectural decisions supporting large-scale ML workloads, leveraging Infrastructure as Code (IaC), particularly using Terraform. 
Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines, ensuring efficient model integration, deployment, and continuous delivery.
Build and optimise monitoring, alerting and logging to ensure model reliability, performance and compliance.
Collaborate closely with data scientists and stakeholders to identify infrastructure needs, streamline workflows, and effectively communicate complex technical concepts.
Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior MLOps engineers and data scientists to promote best practices in ML infrastructure.
What you will bring: 


Essential experience

5+ years of experience in MLOps, DevOps, Data Engineering and/or cloud infrastructure roles, preferably supporting data science or Machine Learning teams.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
Expert proficiency in cloud infrastructure management using Terraform.
Deep hands-on experience with major cloud platforms (AWS, Azure or GCP).
Strong experience in building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines specifically for ML workloads.
Proficiency with containerisation technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).
Advanced proficiency in Python and scripting for infrastructure automation.
Bonus points if you also have:

Experience within iGaming.
Experience working with large volumes of data, preferably at petabyte-scale.
Extensive experience with distributed computing and big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop).
Familiarity with monitoring and observability platforms.
Knowledge of data security, governance, and compliance practices relevant to ML operations.
Some of the perks of joining us:

Access to over 9,000 courses across our Learning and Development Platform.
EAP access for you and your family.
Be rewarded with lucrative annual bonuses.
Give back with a paid volunteer day.
Fuel your day with daily breakfast and open pantries brimming with unlimited snacks and refreshments, all on the house.
Break up the week with on site remedial massage Wednesdays.
In house full-time barista’s providing you your daily coffee needs.
Weekly team lunches and happy hour in the office from 4pm on Fridays.
Enjoy a bustling office with the option for up to 2 days work from home per week.
Fun office environment with F1 simulators, table tennis and all your favourite gaming consoles.

We believe that the unique contributions of everyone at Easygo are the driver of our success. To make sure that our products and culture continue to incorporate everyone's perspectives and experience we never discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or marital, veteran, or disability status. We are passionate about providing a workplace that encourages great participation and an equal playing field, where merit and accomplishment are the only criteria for success.

Build. Iterate. Scale.


Temple & Webster is where design meets technology. As Australia’s leading online retailer for furniture and homewares, we aren’t just selling products; we are reimagining how people discover and shop for their homes. To do that, we need people who view AI as a craft, not just a toolset.


We are expanding our AI capability and are looking for an AI Implementation Engineer to join the team immediately. If you thrive on "what if," enjoy aggressive prototyping, and are driven to relentlessly iterate to find the right solution, we’d like to meet you.


The Role

This isn't a role for a siloed developer. At Temple & Webster, AI is integrated into our core operations. You will have a front-row seat to how a high-growth retailer functions, with the autonomy to identify friction points and build the agentic solutions that solve them.


You’ll be moving beyond "chat" to build autonomous agents that execute real-world tasks - from supply chain logic to hyper-personalised customer journeys.


What we’re looking for:

We value a curious mind and a builder’s mindset over a rigid checklist. We’re interested in:
Commercial Curiosity: You don’t just wait for a brief. You explore our ecosystem, identify where AI can remove manual toil, and build the prototypes to prove it.
The "Ship and Refine" Mentality: We move fast. You should be comfortable with rapid experimentation - learning quickly from each iteration and refining until a solution is production-ready.
Stakeholder Collaboration: You can bridge the gap between complex engineering and business impact. You’ll partner with teams across T&W to understand their challenges and co-create solutions.
Technical Depth: You bring a deep, practical background in generative AI, with a track record of building in commercial environments or via complex personal projects. You possess expert-level fluency in Python, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and the Google AI SDK. More importantly, you understand that while the model is key, the real work lies in the 80%: the data engineering, rigorous evaluation, and LLMOps required to move a project from demo to production.
What you’ll be doing:
Agentic Workflows: Designing AI agents that don’t just talk, but do - integrating them into our core operational systems to remove manual toil.
Hardening AI: Moving beyond the "demo" phase to build high-availability GenAI features with rigorous evaluation frameworks and observability.
Iterating quickly: Prototyping new ideas aggressively while maintaining the security and architectural standards required for enterprise-scale deployment.
Why Temple & Webster?
Real Impact: We’re a tight-knit team where your code directly influences how millions of Australians shop.
Engineering Culture: We believe in the right tool for the job. You'll have the space to experiment with the latest in the AI stack (LangGraph, DSPy, etc.) to solve real retail problems.
The T&W Way: Our culture is built on our people. We provide a beautiful workspace in St Peters, hybrid flexibility, catered lunches three days a week, and a genuine focus on wellbeing (including daily mindfulness and birthday leave) amongst many benefits.

Ready to build?
If you’re a maker who obsesses over the details, we’d love to hear from you. Hit 'Apply' to start the conversation.

P.S. We love seeing what you’ve built. If you have a portfolio, GitHub, or a personal project you’re proud of, please include a link in your application or be ready to walk us through it during the process!

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

Working on the AIR6500 project, the Hardware Engineer will provide services including but not limited to:

• Contribute to the AIR6500 Hardware solution and interfaces in the architectural model to the AIR6500 solution

• Assess Hardware and non-functional related requirements, conduct product trades, define the sell-off approach

• Work with Integrated Product Teams (IPTs) and System Architects to establish hardware requirements and sizing for AIR6500 elements

• Contribute to the development of key AIR6500 technical documents and artefacts such as solution description, trade studies and definition of requirements

• Work with a broad set of stakeholders, across the Lockheed Martin Business and partners for the achievement of common goals

 Responsibilities

The Hardware Engineer Senior (Mechanical)’s primarily responsibilities include:

·         Develop AIR6500 deployable elements and ruggedized solutions for packaging hardware components

·         Establish the product baseline, working with CM/DM team to manage the components, parts and assemblies within Configuration Management Tool

·         Author technical documentation and develop Technical Data packs as required

·         Contribute to the development and conduct of Technical Reviews

·         Define AIR6500 solution accounting for physical interfaces to accommodate future AIR6500 solutions and other key external projects in architecture model

·         Assess physical infrastructure related requirements (map to architecture product trades, sell off approach)

·         Decompose and capture sub-system requirements within the Requirements management tool, DNG. Including traceability and verification methodology

·         Develop Test Plans, Procedures and Test reports as required

·         Conduct design activities, Generate the 3D model and conduct the FEA/CFD analysis as required

·         Engage with AIR6500 IPTs and Specialty Engineering groups to ensure common understanding of requirements and design considerations

·         Perform Trade Studies to evaluate possible technical substitutions as required

About you:

To be successful in this role you will also have:

Knowledge of packaging for ruggedized deployable land-based systems

·         Excellent analysis skills with an ability to develop and evaluate alternatives to determine best value based on customer provided performance weighting

·         Understanding of Model based Systems development and model-based Engineering principles

·         Proficient in concept development

·         Excellent verbal communication skills including both speaking and listening to the customer

·         Familiarity with operational analysis and modelling and simulation

Desirable

Extensive knowledge of:

·         Power Generation and Distribution

·         Environmental Control Systems

·         Hydraulic Systems

·         Electromechanical Control Systems

·         3D model analysis tools such as Ansys

·         Model-based System engineering experience (Rhapsody experience)

·         Requirement decomposition (DNG experience)

·         Ground-based Sensors

·         Ground based Effectors

Job Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer / AI Engineer

About the Role This is an opportunity to join a high-impact AI team building real, customer-facing products at scale. You’ll work across recommendation systems, dynamic pricing engines, and emerging GenAI and agent-based applications, helping shape how intelligent systems are delivered to millions of users.

Small, high-performing team with fast decision-making and real ownership

Your work matters at eHealth NSW
As the digital centre of excellence for NSW Health, we design and deliver secure, scalable technology that supports patient care across the state, helping clinicians provide better healthcare, now and into the future.

Join eHealth NSW to create real-world impact, drive meaningful outcomes and support the health of millions every day.

Join the Team
We are seeking a motivated and skilled Engineer – Hybrid Compute to support the design, deployment, and ongoing operations of hybrid IT infrastructure that seamlessly integrates on-premises systems with public cloud environments.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with senior engineers, cloud architects, and IT operations teams to ensure high performance, security, and operational efficiency across diverse platforms. This position offers an excellent opportunity for an infrastructure or cloud engineer with a strong technical foundation in virtualisation, cloud services, and automation to grow within a forward-thinking and innovative organisation.
 

In this role, you will:

Assist in implementing and maintaining hybrid infrastructure across on-premises and public cloud platforms (Azure and AWS).
Manage and support Windows Server and Unix/Linux systems, ensuring stability, performance, and security.
Administer virtualisation platforms (VMware, Hyper-V), including compute, storage, and networking.

Drive automation using Terraform/Ansible and scripting (PowerShell, Python, Bash) to improve reliability and repeatability.

Manage server patching processes via SCCM and support enterprise print services.
Monitor, troubleshoot and optimise systems and services, collaborating with security teams, maintaining documentation, and providing Tier 2/3 on-call support.

This role is internally referred to as Engineer - DevOps

About You
We are looking for:

Demonstrated working knowledge of systems, infrastructure, or cloud engineering roles.
Operating systems: strong hands-on Windows Server administration and solid capability across Unix/Linux environments.
Virtualisation and cloud: experience with VMware technologies including Hyper-V, vSphere, ESXi, vCenter plus exposure to Azure and AWS (CI/CD and Automation).
Automation and scripting: proficiency with Terraform (and ideally Ansible) and scripting in PowerShell, Python, and/or Bash.
Core infrastructure fundamentals: networking fundamentals, SCCM patch management, endpoint security (Microsoft Defender), and familiarity with enterprise print services.
Ways of working: familiarity with DevOps/Agile practices and container technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).
Soft skills: strong analytical and problem-solving ability, excellent written and verbal communication, a collaborative mindset, and a proactive willingness to learn and adapt.

Drive automation across a national business. Hands-on role supporting the design and delivery of solutions using Microsoft technologies and AI within business workflows.

About Us

RSEA Safety is Australia’s largest independent safety business, with close to 100 stores nationally across retail, B2B and Hire. As part of our continued growth, we’re investing in systems, automation and technology to improve how we operate across the business.

The Role

This role sits within IT and focuses on identifying, designing and supporting the delivery of automation solutions across the business.

At its core, this is a Business Analyst-led role — working closely with stakeholders to understand processes, identify inefficiencies and translate these into practical automation solutions. While AI forms part of the toolkit, the focus is on applying it within workflows rather than building standalone AI products.

You’ll be involved from initial discovery through to implementation, working across multiple departments including operations, finance, customer service, planning and other business areas, while interfacing with core ERP systems and existing platforms.

Key Responsibilities

Work with stakeholders across the business to identify opportunities for automation and process improvement

Facilitate discovery sessions and gather requirements from end users

Map end-to-end processes and identify where automation or AI can add value

Translate business requirements into solution designs and implementation documentation

Build and support automation workflows using Microsoft technologies (Power Platform, Azure)

Support the application of AI tools within workflows, including use cases such as document processing, chat automation and data-driven decision support

Assist with testing, rollout and ongoing support of automation initiatives

Monitor and maintain automation solutions, investigating issues where required

Work closely with IT and ERP teams to support system integration and data flows

Contribute to a pipeline of automation initiatives across multiple business functions

About You

Proven experience as a Business Analyst, Systems Analyst or Automation Analyst

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Experience supporting or delivering process improvement or automation initiatives

Hands-on and detail-oriented, with the ability to work across both business and technical teams

Exposure to Microsoft technologies (Power Automate, Power Apps, Azure or similar)

Practical experience using AI tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, Claude, Gemini or similar) to support automation, analysis or workflow improvements

Understanding of how AI can be applied within business workflows, with exposure to implementing or supporting AI-enabled solutions

Experience in retail, wholesale or similar operational environments is highly regarded

Experience working with ERP systems is necessary

If this sounds like an opportunity of interest, please apply below or contact joshua.boyd@rseasafety.com.au for a confidential discussion.

The Role:
Are you passionate about turning data into a real-world impact? Join us as a Senior Data Engineer and help lead a new wave of analytics innovation. You'll be a key part of a dedicated project team delivering high-value, MVP-driven solutions. This role is responsible for advancing our data science practices, from model development to deployment and governance. 
 
What's in it for you? 

Build and deploy statistical models and advanced analytics to solve real business challenges
Influence and uplift our end-to-end data science capability
Work with modern Azure-based platforms
Collaborate with talented teams across analytics, business intelligence, actuarial, data engineering, and business departments
Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong purpose and community focus
Flexible hybrid work arrangement with work from home and in-office
 
Skills & Experience:

For Senior positions - Minimum 7 years' experience as a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer in a commercial setting.
Bachelor's or higher degree in computer science, information systems, data/analytics engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Proven ability to design, build, and productionise scalable ELT pipelines and analytics datasets (batch and streaming) using modern tooling.
Experience maturing data platforms and engineering practices, including standards for modelling, testing, code review, CI/CD, and governance.
Expertise with Google Cloud data stack—e.g. BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataform or dbt (for ELT), Cloud Composer (Airflow), Dataflow/Beam, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run—and strong familiarity with Looker semantic modelling (LookML).
Experience with Azure platforms is desirable. (Data Factories, Fabric Services)

Strong SQL (performance tuning, partitioning/clustering) and dimensional/data-vault modelling skills.
Highly proficient in Python for data engineering (packaging, testing, virtual environments); familiarity with R an advantage where needed for analytics handoffs.
Solid understanding of security, cost optimisation, observability (logging/metrics/alerts), and data quality frameworks (tests, CI checks, SLAs/SLIs).
 
Highly Desirable  

Experience in Insurance or Banking 
Power BI skills 
Microsoft Azure Certifications 
 
The Company:
Established since 1953 offering stability and security, a great place to grow and develop your career. Having a reputation for excellence you will be able to utilise your skills on challenging and meaningful projects, supported by other subject matter experts within our own collaborative and supportive team.  You will be joining a team from various walks of life accompanied by a broad range of skillsets in this highly collaborative environment where teamwork is our most highly valued asset.  
 
It is essential that you demonstrate in your career, proven experience in your field of expertise. We need forward thinkers, individuals to provide substantiated innovative thinking and solutions to the business.

About Us:

Blackbook.ai is an Australian founded company specialising in Artificial Intelligence, Automation, DataOps, Digital and Analytics. We are passionate about helping organisations unlock their true value, with a proven track record across more than 180 successful client engagements.

Focused on leveraging and nurturing local talent, we provide Australian companies with access to skilled consultants who deliver innovative solutions to complex business challenges. Headquartered in Brisbane with additional offices in Sydney and Melbourne, Blackbook.ai now operates across more than 8 cities globally, collaborating with prominent organisations to drive efficiency, excellence, and measurable outcomes through cutting-edge technology.

About the Team Your are Joining:

You'll be joining our GenAI and Applied AI team. Our focus is building production AI solutions for large Australian enterprises and government clients, with most of our work centred on GenAI and applied AI: RAG systems, document intelligence, agents, and LLM-backed workflows that need to hold up under real data volumes, security requirements, and operational load. We take AI from prototype to production. That means hardening, scaling, deploying, and handing over systems clients can actually run, along with greenfield delivery end-to-end when clients bring us the problem rather than the POC.

About You:

Senior, hands-on delivery role within the GenAI / Applied AI team, leading production GenAI work for large Australian enterprise and government clients. You'd architect, build, and deploy RAG systems, agents, document intelligence, and LLM-backed workflows — typically taking a POC/MVP to production (security, scale, observability, evaluation) or leading greenfield delivery end-to-end, while owning the client relationship through scoping, build, and handover. Also mentors juniors and contributes to internal patterns/accelerators.

Must have: 5+ years across SWE/data/ML/AI with the last 12–24 months in production GenAI; strong Python + API/service dev; production experience on Azure and/or AWS; consulting delivery chops (scoping, workshops, stakeholder management); daily use of AI coding tools; CS/SWE/DS degree or equivalent; full Australian working rights.

Nice to have: regulated-industry delivery, Databricks/GCP, agent frameworks (LangGraph, Semantic Kernel, AutoGen), eval-led development, full-stack/DevOps breadth.

Location: Brisbane preferred; Sydney/Melbourne considered. Some travel to client sites.

What We Offer:

Applied AI work with large Australian enterprises and government clients, on problems that go into production.

A team that takes production quality seriously: testing, evaluation, observability, and proper handover.

Direct client engagement. You'll be working with clients, not relaying tickets.

Investment in certifications (Azure, AWS, Databricks) and time for internal R&D.

Competitive remuneration, commensurate with experience.

Flexible working conditions and a collaborative, technically strong team.

Free Spotify Accounts

50% off Gym memberships

Additional leave entitlements after 2 years

Job Description

The Azure Data Solutions Architect leads the design and implementation of enterprise-scale data solutions using Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, and Azure Synapse Analytics. This role provides architectural leadership, ensuring solutions are robust, scalable, and aligned with business strategy and industry best practices. The architect drives innovation, enforces governance, and mentors' teams to deliver high-quality, future-ready data platforms that support organizational transformation.




Key Responsibilities

1. Provide Architectural Leadership by Designing End-To-End Data Integration and Analytics Solutions Using Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, And Azure Synapse Analytics, Ensuring Alignment with Business Objectives and Compliance with Architectural Standards.


2. Define And Enforce Governance, Security, And Compliance Frameworks for Azure Data Solutions, Leveraging Azure Devops for Automated Deployment, Monitoring, And Quality Assurance.


3. Collaborate With Business Stakeholders to Gather Requirements and Translate Them into Scalable Azure Data Architectures, Optimizing Data Pipelines and Workflows for Performance and Reliability.


4. Review And Validate Architecture and Design Deliverables, Ensuring Adherence to Best Practices in Azure Data Engineering and Continuous Improvement of Solution Quality.


5. Drive Knowledge Upgradation by Evaluating Emerging Azure Technologies, Establishing Standards, And Mentoring Teams on Advanced Data Engineering Practices and Platform Capabilities.


6. Lead The Development and Implementation of Reusable Data Assets, Frameworks, And Accelerators to Streamline Delivery and Ensure Consistency Across Projects.




Skill Requirements

1. Architectural Leadership in Azure Data Factory (Adf), Azure Databricks, And Azure Synapse Analytics, With Expert Ability to Design and Govern Complex Data Solutions.


2. Advanced Proficiency in Azure Devops for Ci/Cd Automation, InfrastructureAsCode, And Deployment of Data Pipelines.


3. Excellent Understanding of Data Modeling, Etl/Elt Processes, Data Governance, And Security Within the Azure Ecosystem.


4. Strong Expertise in Designing Scalable, High-performance Data Architectures for Analytics and Reporting.


5. Solid Knowledge of Industry Best Practices for Cloud Data Platforms, Including Compliance, Monitoring, And Optimization




Other Requirements

1. Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert (Recommended)
2. Microsoft Certified: Azure Data Engineer Associate (Optional But Valuable

Job Title:       Data & Integration Engineer

Company:      Moco Food Services

Location:       Wacol, QLD, 4076, AU

 

·         Own and improve the systems integrations and data flows that power a growing Australian food service business.

·         Design scalable integration solutions across customers, suppliers, and internal platforms.

·         Work directly with operational and senior leaders to deliver real business impact.

 

Step into a role where technology, data and operations meet. This is an opportunity to shape how systems talk to each other, improve the quality of information across the business, and enable smarter decisions every day.

 

About the Role

 

The Data & Integration Engineer plays a critical role within Moco’s IT team, sitting at the intersection of technology and the business. Based in Wacol and reporting to the Capability Development Manager, this role owns the design, development and continuous improvement of system integrations, data flows and reporting across the organisation.

 

From managing customer and supplier EDI integrations through to designing scalable system architecture and improving reporting capability, this role ensures the right information moves to the right place at the right time. By partnering closely with operational and commercial teams, the Data & Integration Engineer helps translate real business needs into practical and reliable technical solutions.

 

 

About You

 

You are someone who enjoys solving complex problems and building systems that work seamlessly together. You bring strong technical capability, particularly in SQL and systems integration, but equally enjoy working with stakeholders to understand operational challenges and turn them into scalable solutions.

 

You thrive in environments where technology enables improvement. Whether it is automating processes, improving data reliability, or designing smarter integrations, you enjoy delivering outcomes that make the business run better.

 

Skills, Experience & Attributes

 

·         Strong SQL (DB2,Mssql) capability including query optimisation and data manipulation.

·         Experience designing and supporting systems integrations across multiple platforms.

·         Experience working with data pipelines, ETL processes.

·         Experience supporting both internal and external system integrations.

·         Experience managing or supporting EDI integrations with customers and suppliers.

·         Hands-on experience with reporting and data platforms such as Power BI.

·         Experience working with GoAnywhere MFT is highly regarded.

·         Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capability.

·         Ability to translate business requirements into practical technical solutions.

·         Comfortable working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 

Benefits

 

·         Work within Moco’s brand new, state-of-the-art facility in Wacol, with plenty of on-site parking available

·         Collaborative and friendly workplace environment, with weekly cross-departmental events

·         Join a growing company and use your expertise to make a real impact on the business

·         Leadership training and development opportunities available for aspiring employees

·         Access to great discounts on over 6,500 product items for current employees.

·         Enjoy free access to financial advice, home loan support, and exclusive lifestyle perks & discounts through our partnership with Commonwealth Bank.

 

Moco – Delivering Inspiration!

 

Moco exists for one reason. To help our customers run great food businesses. No shortcuts. No politics.  We play the long game.

 

We deliver world-class food logistics and menu expertise, but what truly sets us apart is how we show up every day. We think differently. We move fast. We innovate. We back our customers to win. Our brand matters. Our people turn up with energy!

 

We are proud to say that we are a privately owned Australian business. Agile enough to move quickly. Bold enough to challenge convention. Ambitious enough to build something extraordinary. When our customers win, we win. When they grow, we grow.

 

We have a brand-new state-of-the-art facility and industry-leading technology, but our true advantage isn’t something you can buy; it’s our people and the legacy they create. Driven teams who care deeply about our customers and each other.

 

Join us on our journey as we embark on an exciting new phase of growth. We have established a solid foundation, and the next stage of our expansion is just beginning. With strong momentum and a clear vision, we are creating a food service company designed for scalability and impact. Our approach is rooted in integrity, driven by innovation, and supported by a team of people dedicated to making a difference.

 

How to Apply

 

To submit your application, click the QUICK APPLY button on this page, or contact Travis Gibson – Talent Acquisition Manager – on tgibson@mocofoodservices.com.au

Analytics Engineer


Supp is a fast-growing jobs platform connecting over 200,000 hospitality professionals with more than 8,000 independent venues across Australia and the US. By harnessing real-time data and technology, we're tackling some of the industry's toughest staffing challenges - making life easier for both workers and venues. As we continue to grow, we're looking for talented people to join us and help shape the future of hospitality.

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to work within our ML and Analytics team - someone skilled at helping others extract meaningful insight from data and make a real impact on the future of our company.

In this position, you'll work across our BigQuery Data Mesh and BI systems, helping create and maintain a core suite of reports, and maintaining the DBT pipelines that fuel our BI, and ML systems. You'll also help drive our Data Platform, ensuring that our data is secure, high quality, and ready to be accessed efficiently to guide business decisions.

We are not looking for an Excel specialist, but someone with experience with fully-fledged Data Platforms and is comfortable with BI systems such as Data Studio, or Tableau.

About the platform

Supp is a dynamic two-sided marketplace connecting businesses and hospitality professionals in real time.

We use data-driven matching to pair the right candidates with the right opportunities.

More than just a jobs board, we have created a platform that offers management of worker profiles, business listings, payments, reviews & ratings, administration and community.

We've only scratched the surface of what's possible. Our growth has given us a clear view of the huge opportunities ahead - and we're building fast to make them happen.

The way we work
Size

As a small team, we hire productive, capable people so that we can stay lean and efficient. Fewer meetings, fewer committees. More time building for our customers!

Teamwork

As a company we strive to make data-driven decisions. You'll collaborate across the business to deliver insights that shape our product, and enable confident decisions

Empowerment

At Supp, we believe that a small way we can improve the world is by striving for equality. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. If you require assistance at any stage of the application process, please let us know.

Applying

What We’re Looking For

If you enjoy writing code with AI tools, digitising real-world workflows, presenting technology to clients, and being part of a team where what you build genuinely matters, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Senior Specialist Engineer – Vehicle and Mechanical Systems
Ad Text:
The Role

As the EL2 Senior Specialist Engineer, Vehicle and Mechanical Systems, in the Directorate of Specialist Engineering, you will lead a small team of highly specialised engineers and technical staff who are focused on the assessment and enhancement of Land materiel, to assure the safety and performance of vehicle systems used by the ADF.

You will apply your significant engineering expertise to assess very complex engineering problems in the field of vehicle and mechanical systems. This could include coordination of physical testing and evaluation, and undertaking modelling and analytical assessment of vehicle system performance. Your work may require applying specialist skills and experience such as vehicle dynamics and off-road mobility, vibration analysis, finite element modelling, crashworthiness and structural integrity, and equipment transportability.

You will be responsible for guiding, mentoring and developing your team members, and as the Vehicle and Mechanical Systems Community of Practice Lead, you will be expected to drive capability growth and ongoing professional development of Land Systems Division members in this field of practice.

About our Team

Land Systems Division’s (LSD) mission is to acquire and support capability that enables the ADF to generate and sustain deployable Land power for an integrated force, in peace and war. We aim to be agile, innovative and outcomes-focussed as we collaborate closely with the Services and industry to deliver the latest equipment to maximise ADF performance.  

The Land Engineering Agency (LEA) within LSD, supports various projects and Systems Program Offices (SPO) to ensure successful technical outcomes of Defence acquisition and in-service support of land materiel. LEA provides services in engineering, testing, design and development, technical data and configuration management across the Defence capability life-cycle.  

LSD is dedicated to providing staff with an inclusive and healthy work-life environment, and we will invest in your ongoing professional development to ensure your work with us is deeply rewarding.    

More information about the Land systems we support can be found at: https://www.defence.gov.au/projects#land

Our Ideal Candidate

The ideal candidate will be an experienced engineering professional, with extensive knowledge of engineering methodology, systems and procedures, particularly relating to mechanical aspects of very complex military vehicle platforms.

You will lead independently, and with your growth mindset and depth of experience, you manage resources and capacity effectively, while mentoring and inspiring team members to nurture talent and grow the technical and engineering capability of your team.

You will provide robust strategic and specialist advice, clearly, concisely and articulately, to a wide range of senior leaders and stakeholders. Your demonstrated ability to critically analyse very complex problems and develop workable solutions, together with sound judgement and decision-making, will enable you to adapt quickly to stakeholder requirements and influence where required.

Application Closing Date: Sunday 10 May 2026

For further information please review the job information pack, reference CASG/02210/26 on https://defencecareers.nga.net.au/?jati=C95572BD-D341-EAFB-2C3C-ECDA045E48D8

Job Title: Senior Architect - Data and AI Engineering
Ad Text:
Slalom is a fiercely human business and technology consulting company that leads with outcomes to bring more value, in all ways, always. From strategy through delivery, our agile teams across 52 offices in 12 countries collaborate with clients to bring powerful customer experiences, innovative ways of working, and new products and services to life. We are trusted by leaders across the Global 1000, many successful enterprise and mid-market companies, and 500+ public sector organisations to improve operations, drive growth, and create value. At Slalom, we believe that together, we can move faster, dream bigger, and build better tomorrows for all. 

Senior Architect - Data and AI Engineering

The purpose of our Data and AI practice at Slalom is to build a better future with Data. Through trusted partnerships with our clients, we co-create modern data solutions that drive results and improve the world. We are continuing to grow our practice whilst keeping Diversity, Equity, and Inclusiveness at the forefront, and hiring individuals who share a similar passion for data, technology, and culture. 

What You Will Do

·    Work in small, collaborative teams with minimal oversight and direction to design and deliver innovative solutions on Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform using core cloud data warehouse tools, distributed processing engines, event streaming platforms, and other modern data technologies.

·    Build the next generation of data platforms and work with some of the most forward-thinking organizations in data and analytics.

·    Responsible for understanding the client’s current environment and technical challenges as well as their plans for evolving or creating their future state architecture.

·    Using your personal experiences, architecture expertise and your consultative skills, you will facilitate working design sessions with Slalom and Client technical teams to drive the strategy, architecture, and implementation of a data engineering solution.

·    Effectively describe the pros and cons of your solution design and drive the combined Slalom and client team to a consensus.

·    Breaking down complex development tasks and will be responsible for the timely quality completion of development items assigned to you and the development team reporting to you, ensuring that they support the Technical Solution you designed.

·    As time allows, you will participate in pre-sales activities including helping define an approach to solving a prospect’s technical challenges and helping the business development team estimate and plan projects to address those challenges.

What You Will Bring

·    Broad experience in architecture design, a deep expertise in one or more data engineering technologies (listed below), a curious mindset to your client’s engagement, a thirst for knowledge, and a hunger for fearless experimentation in new and interesting ways to meet our clients’ most pressing data challenges.

·    Self-starter mindset and ability effectively break down large problems into smaller ones and regularly share your learnings within your projects and in the broader Slalom Build community.

·    Hands-on experience with new data platforms and programming languages as you explore the range of technologies that we help our clients with including:

·    Big Data Platforms (Apache Spark, Presto, Amazon EMR) Cloud Data Warehouses (Amazon Redshift, Snowflake, Google BigQuery)

·    Object Oriented Coding (Java, Python)

·    NoSQL Databases (DynamoDB, Cosmos DB, MongoDB)

·    Container Management Systems (Kubernetes, Amazon ECS)

·    Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning (Amazon Sagemaker, Azure ML Studio)

·    Streaming Data Ingestion and Analytics (Amazon Kinesis, Apache Kafka)

·    Visual Analytics (Tableau, PowerBI)

·    Modern Data Workflows (Apache Airflow, dbt, Dagster).

Our Values 

Our culture and values reflect each individual Slalomer. We are dedicated to encouraging workplace diversity and ensuring all our people, from any background can contribute to their full potential. We wish to build a team that echoes the diversity of the communities we serve, and our values are an active part of our every-day, guiding our decisions. 

What keeps us here? 

When we moved to Australia in 2020, we had no idea just how important our work/life balance would need to be. We’ve asked our consultants what makes Slalom a great place to work and the most common answer – “the freedom to be ourselves when life gets in the way”. We make a big effort to bring everyone together and allow space when space is needed. We believe in helping each other thrive in work and life and are proud to invest in benefits that include holistic wellbeing, meaningful allowances, and investment in our team’s professional, technical, and personal growth. 

Slalom is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes all qualified applicants. Slalom does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion, or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other basis as protected by applicable law.

Why SoftwareOne?

SoftwareOne and Crayon have come together to form a global, AI-powered software and cloud solutions provider with a bold vision for the future. With a footprint in over 70 countries and a diverse team of 13,000+ professionals, we offer unparalleled opportunities for talent to grow, make an impact, and shape the future of technology. At the heart of our business is our people. We empower our teams to work across borders, innovate fearlessly, and continuously develop their skills through world-class learning and development programs. Whether you're passionate about cloud, software, data, AI, or building meaningful client relationships, you’ll find a place to thrive here. Join us and be part of a purpose-driven culture where your ideas matter, your growth is supported, and your career can go global. 


The role

Passionate about leading high‑impact machine learning initiatives and delivering scalable, production‑ready AI solutions?

Motivated to mentor and grow a team of ML and data engineers while remaining hands‑on with advanced model development and deployment?

Inspired to join a collaborative Data & AI organisation that values innovation, technical excellence, and continuous learning?

As our new Machine Learning Engineer – Team Lead, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design and delivery of machine learning solutions end‑to‑end, while providing technical leadership and mentorship to a small, high‑performing engineering team. You will bridge business objectives with advanced ML techniques, guide architectural decisions, and ensure models are production‑ready, scalable, and continuously improved.

Key responsibilities will include:

Lead and mentor a team of ML and Data Engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and supporting professional development.
Own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of machine learning initiatives, translating business requirements into well‑defined ML problem statements, technical solutions, and delivery plans.
Design, build, train, and evaluate advanced machine learning models, and contribute to architectural decisions for scalable ML pipelines and cloud‑based deployments (Azure preferred).
Partner with MLOps and DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines, model versioning, monitoring, retraining, and production support best practices.
Collaborate cross‑functionally with Data Engineering, Software Development, Sales & Pre‑Sales, and leadership stakeholders, while staying current with emerging ML technologies and driving adoption where valuable.

What we need to see from you
Proven experience delivering machine learning solutions into production environments.
Strong technical expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Scikit‑learn.
Solid understanding of end‑to‑end ML pipelines, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model evaluation, and monitoring.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred), Docker, Kubernetes, and MLOps tooling (e.g., MLflow, Airflow).
Ability to balance technical depth with leadership responsibilities and commercial awareness and having experience working within agile delivery environments and across multiple stakeholder groups

Job Function

Software & Cloud
